message:
chore(beads): close ft-zo3cr partial + file ft-bkgbq

mcp_tools.rs drift fix landed in ddf4eb8f. main.rs's 4 sites
(:6992/:15341/:19850/:25526) + the :19850-vs-:25526 config-skew (missing
command_gate_config + policy_rules at :25526) filed as ft-bkgbq with
the concrete factory-bundle shape per the bead's original suggestion.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Narrow lane used: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-MistyCoast-target CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test wa_state_mcp_conformance --features mcp,asupersync-runtime. That fell back locally on RCH-E324, then local wrapper reproduced a compile-stop before the new test ran: crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs has non-exhaustive matches for Err(events::RecvError::Cancelled) at lines 574, 586, 749, and 786 (E0004). I stayed inside the assigned slice; only new file is crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs and it remains uncommitted pending baseline compile repair.","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:50:51Z"}]}

Reuse manifest schema freeze; do not touch mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs (pane 2 owns it).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:31:02.808965Z","created_by":"LilacBay","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:00:24.414471Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:00:24.414130Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 01d5e233","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.2","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:31:02.808965Z","created_by":"LilacBay","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3620,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Reopened: Restamping close reason to shipped commit per follow-up","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:59:33Z"},{"id":3621,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Reopened: Restamping close reason to shipped commit per follow-up","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:00:17Z"}]}

Use the mission/tx golden-capture pattern, seed storage directly, and pin success plus invalid-args envelopes for wa.reserve, wa.reservations, and wa.release without touching mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:36:28.848013Z","created_by":"LilacBay","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:19:58.028447Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:19:58.028214Z","close_reason":"Shipped in d4e41dca","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.3","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:36:28.848013Z","created_by":"LilacBay","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3622,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Switching off this bead immediately: LilacBay already holds an exclusive reservation on crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_reservations_mcp_conformance.rs, so this would collide with pane 4. Moving to fallback bead wa-nu4.3.9.19 instead of contending on the same conformance file.","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:14:20Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-4o5mb.4","title":"[conformance] wa.tx_* lacks TOON envelope parity and boundary invalid-params coverage","description":"`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs` already freezes JSON success/error goldens for `wa.tx_plan`, `wa.tx_show`, `wa.tx_run`, and `wa.tx_rollback`, but it does not exercise `format: "toon"` envelopes and it does not pin fastmcp boundary invalid-params behavior for wrong-typed / missing required fields.

Gap: tx MCP callers can regress TOON semantics or input-boundary contracts without any conformance failure, even though the schemas advertise json/toon negotiation and typed inputs in `tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`.

Fix: add conformance captures that compare TOON vs JSON semantic envelopes and freeze one boundary invalid-params response per tool.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:20:25.357797Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:48:15.507060Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:20:25.357797Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-4o5mb.4","title":"[conformance] wa.tx_* lacks TOON envelope parity and boundary invalid-params coverage","description":"`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs` already freezes JSON success/error goldens for `wa.tx_plan`, `wa.tx_show`, `wa.tx_run`, and `wa.tx_rollback`, but it does not exercise `format: "toon"` envelopes and it does not pin fastmcp boundary invalid-params behavior for wrong-typed / missing required fields.

Gap: tx MCP callers can regress TOON semantics or input-boundary contracts without any conformance failure, even though the schemas advertise json/toon negotiation and typed inputs in `tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`.

Fix: add conformance captures that compare TOON vs JSON semantic envelopes and freeze one boundary invalid-params response per tool.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:20:25.357797Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:48.933217Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:48.933049Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 0f744567: add wa.tx TOON conformance harness (verify partial)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:20:25.357797Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-4o5mb.5","title":"[conformance] wa.mission_* lacks TOON envelope parity and boundary invalid-params coverage","description":"`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs` already freezes JSON success/error goldens for `wa.mission_state`, `wa.mission_explain`, `wa.mission_pause`, `wa.mission_resume`, and `wa.mission_abort`, but it does not exercise `format: "toon"` envelopes and it does not pin fastmcp boundary invalid-params behavior for wrong-typed / missing required fields.

Gap: mission MCP callers can regress TOON semantics or input-boundary contracts without any conformance failure, even though the tool schemas advertise json/toon negotiation and typed inputs in `tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`.

Fix: add per-tool conformance capture that (a) asserts TOON envelope parity against the JSON success path and (b) records the framework invalid-params response for one representative wrong-typed required field per tool.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:20:55.455807Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:47:44.068592Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:47:13.917290Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 62744f55: add wa.mission TOON conformance harness (verify partial)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:20:55.455807Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-4qom2","title":"[SEV] tmux cmd_queue cap only enforced at pop site; unbounded growth when state != Idle","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue has CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000 enforced only inside send_next_command at line 289 via cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286-288 when state != Idle, so the drain-oldest logic never runs if tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse.

Four push sites append to cmd_queue with NO cap check:
- line 196: Event::LayoutChange -> ListAllPanes
- line 223: Event::SessionChanged -> ListCommands
- line 234: Event::WindowAdd -> ListAllWindows
- line 375: split_pane (user split) -> SplitPane

Concrete interleaving: (1) tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse; (2) tmux server is slow/hung (real-world scenario: remote tmux on lagging SSH link, tmux server overloaded, Event::Guarded response delayed); (3) GUI user resizes window repeatedly -> stream of Event::LayoutChange -> each push_back at line 196 runs without cap check; (4) send_next_command early-returns because state != Idle. Queue grows unbounded -> memory leak, eventual OOM.

The CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH comment at line 32-33 explicitly advertises 'prevent unbounded memory growth during event storms' as a safety invariant. That invariant is currently only upheld at the pop site, where it can't run under the exact condition (stuck tmux) that event storms exploit.

Fix: extract the cap-enforcement logic into a push_command_capped helper that applies the drain-oldest + warn/error logging at every push site. The helper runs under the cmd_queue lock (just acquired by the caller), so it's atomic with the push and has no additional locking cost.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:17:26.601099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.160715Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.158111Z","close_reason":"Completed: added push_command_capped() helper and routed all 4 production push sites (LayoutChange/SessionChanged/WindowAdd/split_pane) through it. The hard cap is now enforced at push time, not only at pop time — closing the unbounded-growth window when tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse. cargo check passes clean. Commit 1055cffc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4r2ib","title":"[mission][dispatch] MissionDispatcher reports accepted dispatch without calling WorkflowRunner::handle_detection","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs:200-226`, `dispatch_single()` builds a synthetic detection, then only calls `runner.find_matching_workflow(&detection)` and fabricates `accepted: true` plus a synthetic `execution_id` when a workflow exists. It never invokes `WorkflowRunner::handle_detection()`, so no lock acquisition, no concurrency-limit enforcement, no storage row, and no real workflow start happen.

Impact: callers receive a success-shaped `DispatchResult` that claims work was accepted even though the runner was never asked to start anything.

Fix direction: bridge the sync mission loop to the async runner start path with a bounded runtime, map the actual `WorkflowStartResult` into `DispatchResult`, and only emit started metadata from a real start result.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:09:03.405716Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:16:34.545322Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:16:34.544924Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 0761bb33: mission dispatch now calls WorkflowRunner::handle_detection and returns real start metadata","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -137,6 +137,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-b9ndv","title":"[fuzz] add MCP request params parser harness","description":"Existing fuzz coverage already hits pattern-pack TOML/binary, config TOML, workflow YAML, robot envelope JSON, IPC auth envelope, snapshot deserialization, replay recordings, and term/escape parsers. But there is still no fuzz target for MCP request-param parsing.

Evidence:
- `fuzz/Cargo.toml` has no MCP parser target; current targets stop at `ipc_auth_envelope`, `robot_envelope_json`, and related config parsers.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_types.rs:25-170` defines the externally-controlled MCP request param structs (`StateParams`, `GetTextParams`, `SearchParams`, `EventsParams`, etc.), but they are `pub(super)` and only covered by unit/proptest deserialization checks.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_types.rs:1170-1845` has property tests over many `serde_json::from_value(...)` paths, but no byte-level fuzzing against malformed/truncated/oversized JSON.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_framework.rs:203-265` also round-trips untrusted remote MCP payloads through `serde_json::from_value(...)`.

Why it matters:
- MCP is a network/external-input surface with many optional/defaulted fields and multiple tool-specific param shapes.
- Proptests cover valid structured values, not adversarial raw JSON bytes, duplicate keys, deep nesting, type confusion, or truncation.

Fix:
- Add a fuzz seam plus harness that feeds raw JSON/Value payloads through the MCP request-param decode paths (or framework payload bridge) and asserts no panic / deterministic decode outcomes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-04-23T19:26:37.331975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:48.039115Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:48.038906Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4561eac1: add MCP request params fuzz harness and seed corpus","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","review"]}
 {"id":"ft-bekwl","title":"[tx][compensation] PaneStepExecutor compensation path returns synthetic success without executing rollback actions","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tx_execution.rs:633-673`, `PaneStepExecutor::execute_compensations()` has no access to the contract compensation plan, leaves `contract_compensations` empty, and returns `success: true` / `reason_code: "compensation_succeeded"` for each committed step unless a deterministic injected failure was requested. The comments explicitly admit this is synthetic reporting rather than real rollback.

Impact: tx rollback can report success even when no compensation action actually ran.

Fix direction: thread contract compensation actions into the executor/execution engine so compensation dispatch can execute real `StepAction`s instead of emitting synthetic success records.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:09:03.406659Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:41.670498Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:41.670191Z","close_reason":"Fully fixed by 74a775bc (ft-npj7t). Verified inspection of tx_execution.rs:629-685: (a) execute_compensations now takes &MissionTxContract, (b) contract_compensations HashMap populated from contract.plan.compensations, (c) real execute_step_action call replaces synthetic success on line 679-682, (d) missing compensation returns fail-closed FTX_COMPENSATION_MISSING, (e) integration test at :3567-3589 asserts mock pane content contains 'ROLLBACK' after rollback (proving end-to-end dispatch). All four elements of the original ft-bekwl claim are now FALSE. Duplicate of ft-npj7t.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-biafh","title":"[audit-note] runtime.rs deadlock-finder no finding","description":"Deadlock-finder-and-fixer pass on crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs completed on 2026-04-23. Reviewed relay/persistence handoff, native-event coalescer, lock ordering, and shutdown/task-join paths. No CRITICAL/HIGH deadlock, starvation, lost-wakeup, or unbounded-buffer defect with a concrete repro was found in runtime.rs during this pass.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:18:02.424603Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:49.325248Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:49.324937Z","close_reason":"Audit note recorded; no finding from deadlock-finder pass on runtime.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-bkgbq","title":"[architecture][follow-up ft-zo3cr] Centralize PolicyEngine + PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowRunner assembly in main.rs","description":"## Context

ft-zo3cr identified an architectural smell: `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` repeats the PolicyEngine + PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowRunner assembly in at least 4 branches with slightly-different defaults:

- :6992-6999 — PolicyEngine::new + with_tuning + with_command_gate_config + with_policy_rules (bare, for a single policy check)
- :15341-15359 — PolicyEngine::permissive + PolicyGatedInjector + CxPolicyInjector + WorkflowEngine + PaneWorkflowLockManager (full workflow runtime stack)
- :19850-19869 — PolicyEngine::new(require_prompt_active) + with_tuning + with_command_gate_config + with_policy_rules (similar to :6992 but with injector+storage)
- :25526-25540 — PolicyEngine::new(true) + with_tuning BUT OMITS with_command_gate_config and with_policy_rules (drift!)

ft-zo3cr in-scope fix (landed in ddf4eb8f) closed a similar drift in mcp_tools.rs:2558. The main.rs sites were left out because this architectural bead scoped only crates/frankenterm-core/* via its reservation.

## Why this matters

The :19850 vs :25526 comparison is the exact kind of drift the bead warns about: one call site gates on command_gate + policy_rules, the other doesn't. Any future fix to one doesn't land in the other. The :19846-19849 comment in current code already records a past regression of this shape (workflow injector diverged from the direct send path).

## Suggested direction (matches the bead's original proposal)

Introduce a single composition factory in frankenterm-core (or a dedicated module under crates/frankenterm/src/) exposing shape-typed builders:

    fn build_human_send_stack(config: &Config) -> HumanSendStack { ... }
    fn build_mcp_workflow_stack(config: &Config, storage: StorageHandle, wezterm: ...) -> McpWorkflowStack { ... }
    fn build_robot_workflow_stack(config: &Config, storage: ...) -> RobotWorkflowStack { ... }

Each returns a bundle (PolicyEngine + Injector + optional Runner). Handlers at the 4 main.rs sites become declarative — they ask for the bundle rather than open-code the assembly.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Each of the 4 main.rs sites uses one of the shared factories.
- The feature set (tuning / command_gate / policy_rules / require_prompt_active) applied by each factory is asserted by a focused unit test (one per bundle shape).
- Any future config knob added to PolicyEngine has exactly ONE place to thread through.

## Scope note

Requires crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs reservation (not in ft-zo3cr's scope). Best picked up after any outstanding main.rs work (e.g. other rate-limiter or safety-config refactors) lands.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing ft-zo3cr's mcp_tools.rs drift fix, 2026-04-23.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:00:10.623144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:00:10.623144Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","cli","follow-up","strategic"]}
 {"id":"ft-bs0ec","title":"robot_types agent inventory golden contract","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T04:22:08.228201Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:56:43.693909Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:56:43.693614Z","close_reason":"Landed in 97ec6350 (test(frankenterm-core): freeze agent_inventory_contract.json golden for robot payload)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-btg0h","title":"[fuzz] add frankenterm/term Terminal::advance_bytes target for full escape-to-state pipeline","description":"Surface: frankenterm/term/src/terminal.rs:164 — pub fn advance_bytes<B: AsRef<[u8]>>(&mut self, bytes: B). Master terminal state-machine entry point: raw output bytes → frankenterm_escape_parser → Performer::perform (terminalstate/performer.rs:252) → every CSI/SGR/OSC/DCS dispatch path (perform_csi_mode/window/edit/cursor/sgr in terminalstate/mod.rs:1425-2648, plus image pipelines in terminalstate/sixel.rs, terminalstate/kitty.rs (37KB), terminalstate/iterm.rs). Existing escape_parser_raw fuzz target covers only the lexer stage (bytes → Action); termwiz_csi_parser covers the escape-parser CSI roundtrip property. Nothing exercises the state-machine mutations, cursor/edit bounds, or the Sixel/Kitty/iTerm2 image decoders under arbitrary input.

Proposed harness: fuzz/fuzz_targets/term_advance_bytes.rs — construct a Terminal with a minimal TerminalConfiguration impl, 24x80 size, cap input at 256 KB, feed the whole slice through advance_bytes, drop the terminal. No assertions — libFuzzer asserts no-panic / no-OOM / no-unbounded-alloc.

Corpus seeds: plain text, ESC[H clear, SGR sequences, DECSET/DECRST modes, DECRQM, OSC 52 clipboard, OSC 8 hyperlinks, bracketed paste, CSI u keyboard, DCS sixel fragment (\\x1bPq#0;2;100;0;0#0~~\\x1b\\\\), kitty APC image fragment, iTerm2 OSC 1337 file upload preamble, overlong / truncated / interleaved-with-text variants.

Target must survive 256 KB input without: panic, unbounded allocation (scrollback bounded at 64), unterminated DCS hang, arithmetic overflow in cursor arithmetic, OOB cell index. This discovers sixel/kitty image-decoder arithmetic bugs and any CSI dispatch path that does not clamp its operand to grid bounds.

Add to fuzz/Cargo.toml: frankenterm-term = { path = "../frankenterm/term" }. No new features required — Terminal::advance_bytes is in the crate public API for terminal hosts.

Do NOT duplicate: ft-t4n0z (termwiz InputParser, closed), ft-he0w7 (native_events, closed), ft-p2bjm (replay, in flight), escape_parser_raw (lexer stage only), termwiz_csi_parser (CSI roundtrip property on escape-parser crate, not term::Terminal).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T07:08:20.022220Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:29:40.992943Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:29:40.992183Z","close_reason":"Landed in 168d379a (term_advance_bytes harness + corpus); fuzz-build linker blocker documented on bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","security"],"comments":[{"id":3618,"issue_id":"ft-btg0h","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented local diff for term_advance_bytes fuzz harness in fuzz/Cargo.toml, fuzz/fuzz_targets/term_advance_bytes.rs, and fuzz/corpus/term_advance_bytes/*.

Build blocker on verification lane:
1. rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-DarkAnchor-target CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ cargo +nightly fuzz build term_advance_bytes
2. rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-DarkAnchor-target CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_AARCH64_APPLE_DARWIN_LINKER=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ cargo +nightly fuzz build term_advance_bytes

Both runs reach the final link for bin term_advance_bytes and fail with the same libfuzzer/c++ symbol error:
Undefined symbols for architecture arm64:
  std::__1::__hash_memory(void const*, unsigned long)
from liblibfuzzer_sys ... FuzzerLoop.o

The second run proves the explicit clang++ linker override does not fix it. Keeping bead open pending toolchain/libfuzzer resolution or alternate verified build lane.","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:20:12Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-zbccp","title":"error renderer typed pane golden fixture missing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:55:19.480893Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:07:40.816754Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:07:40.816449Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-zf98g","title":"README documents ft robot wait-for <rule_id> instead of pattern","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:09:26.532326Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:26:05.552267Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:26:05.551537Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 386df02d","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3607,"issue_id":"ft-zf98g","author":"jemanuel","text":"Reality-check evidence (2026-04-23): README.md still documents ft robot wait-for <id> <rule_id> at README.md:518 and README.md:945-946. The CLI signature is pane_id + pattern, with substring matching by default and regex only when --regex is passed: crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:2458-2472. AGENTS.md already documents the correct shape as ft robot wait-for <pane_id> "pattern" at AGENTS.md:312. Expected: README examples and pattern-ID guidance should say pattern (or pattern plus --regex), not rule_id.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:10:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-znu6v","title":"[audit] storage/fts: deferred retention can strand last_indexed_seq past seq reset","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:33:26.451931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:56:33.770699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:56:33.769747Z","close_reason":"prune_segments_sync rewinds stranded fts_pane_progress; 2 regression tests land in 2099121d (absorbed by concurrent commit)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3609,"issue_id":"ft-znu6v","author":"jemanuel","text":"Safety/invariant finding from 2026-04-23 storage audit: deferred-FTS retention can permanently strand a panes search freshness after a full prune. Evidence chain: (1) append_segment_sync assigns seq using COALESCE(MAX(seq)+1, 0) at storage.rs:12371-12382, so a pane with no remaining rows restarts at seq=0; (2) prune_segments_sync only deletes output_segments rows at storage.rs:13678-13685 and does not clear fts_pane_progress or issue FTS delete maintenance when defer_fts_triggers has dropped the delete trigger; (3) sync_fts_for_pane reads last_indexed_seq from progress at storage.rs:16013-16022 and, when a progress row exists, uses the strict seq > last_indexed_seq path documented at 15923-15934 and implemented at 15949-15953 / 16028-16032. Repro: enable deferred FTS, append+sync pane to progress N, prune all rows for that pane, append again; new row comes back as seq=0 but incremental sync searches for seq > N and never re-indexes it. Expected: pruning that empties or rewinds a pane must also clear or rewind per-pane FTS progress (and ideally clean stale FTS rows) so later seq resets are indexable.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-zo3cr","title":"[architecture] centralize PolicyEngine and WorkflowRunner construction in one composition path","description":"Evidence:
- crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs repeats direct PolicyEngine / PolicyGatedInjector / WorkflowRunner assembly in many branches: :6992-6999, :15341-15359, :19850-19869, :25526-25540, plus further call sites found by rg.
- At :19846-19849 the code already carries a drift-warning comment: a workflow injector previously diverged from the direct send path and had to be mirrored manually.

Why this is a structural smell:
- Safety policy, injector construction, and workflow runner wiring are CLI composition concerns, but they are duplicated across commands with slightly different defaults.
- Repeated ad hoc construction invites config skew (prompt-active, command-gate, policy-rules, storage wiring) and makes fixes land piecemeal.
- Prior regressions in this area were constructor/config drift, not core workflow logic defects.

Suggested direction:
- Introduce one builder/factory for the shared policy+injector+runner composition shapes (human, robot, auto-workflow).
- Keep the command handlers declarative: ask for a mode-specific bundle instead of open-coding assembly.
- Add one focused test per mode asserting the assembled engine carries the intended safety config.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:48:16.066948Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:09.623993Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","strategic"]}

Swapped :2558 to the shared-rate-limiter variant using the policy_rate_limiter already in handler scope. Verified: only remaining build_policy_engine( match in mcp_tools.rs is inside a documentation comment; all 9 non-test call sites consistently use the _with_shared_rate_limiter variant.

Bead's primary scope — main.rs's 4 sites at :6992/:15341/:19850/:25526 with the known :19850-vs-:25526 config drift (command_gate_config + policy_rules missing from the :25526 site) — not in this shipment because main.rs is outside the mcp.rs+mcp_tools.rs+policy.rs reservation. Filed ft-bkgbq with the concrete factory shape (per-mode HumanSendStack / McpWorkflowStack / RobotWorkflowStack bundles) and acceptance criteria pinning each mode's feature set.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","strategic"]}
 {"id":"ft-ztcsl","title":"mux ClientInfo JSON roundtrip truncates subsecond timestamps","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:00:01.250430Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:13:54.131088Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:13:54.130678Z","close_reason":"Fixed: frankenterm/mux/src/client.rs:42-65 ClientInfo now uses '#[serde(with = ts_milliseconds)]' on both connected_at (line 53) and last_input (line 61) instead of the former ts_seconds. Inline comments at lines 47-52 and 59-60 explicitly cite ft-ztcsl. JSON roundtrip now preserves millisecond-resolution timestamps; two clients connecting in the same second no longer coarsen to the same value.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-zu8g3","title":"connector_outbound_bridge::OutboundDeduplicator: capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le, ft-61kg4)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs:596 OutboundDeduplicator::check_and_record with capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. The 'if entries.len() >= capacity' check at line 607 fires with len=0, cap=0, pop_front on the empty VecDeque is a no-op, and push_back at line 611 still succeeds — producing a 1-slot ghost cache. Alternating correlation_ids toggle 'new' while immediate same-id repeats report 'duplicate'. Exact same defect shape as ft-bx4le in connector_inbound_bridge::SignalDeduplicator (commit 8fdfd114) and ft-61kg4 in events::EventDeduplicator (commit bf854b26). Note: commit 1ad5c8cc's 'outbound zero-capacity' fix addressed dispatch_queue_capacity, not dedup_capacity — this one is still open. Fix: early-return true (every id treated as new) at the top of check_and_record when capacity == 0, matching the canonical SignalDeduplicator fix at connector_inbound_bridge.rs:351.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:56:01.113371Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:57:09.504002Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:57:09.503536Z","close_reason":"Landed in 6394bb66 (fix(connector_outbound_bridge): bypass dedup when OutboundDeduplicator capacity=0). Early-return true at top of check_and_record when capacity == 0. Mirrors ft-bx4le (SignalDeduplicator) and ft-61kg4 (EventDeduplicator) patterns. Regression test connector_outbound_bridge_dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup_ft_zu8g3 pins behavior. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-zv3u9","title":"events::Event missing PartialEq; mission_dispatch.rs:542 assert_eq! fails to compile","description":"mission_dispatch.rs:542 uses assert_eq! on crate::events::Event values, but the Event enum in events.rs:146 doesn't derive PartialEq. Rustc suggests #[derive(PartialEq)]. Blocks cargo check/build of frankenterm-core lib. Fix: either derive PartialEq on Event (if all variants are Eq-able) or rewrite the assertion to compare specific fields via pattern match.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:01:54.113665Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:04.478409Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:04.478033Z","close_reason":"Fixed: crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs:542-560 was rewritten to match+destructure Event::WorkflowCompleted instead of assert_eq, with an inline comment citing ft-zv3u9 and the rationale that Event payload includes Detection/UserVarPayload which would cascade PartialEq across a wide surface. Event still doesn't derive PartialEq, and the test now compiles without needing it. Verified by cargo check --lib --tests passing past the prior E0369.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
